events.change_summary: Intent check: extend guided deploy-failure remediation beyond database connection errors

events.summary: Intent check (rev 1): extend guided deploy-failure recovery beyond the database-connection case to cover at least one more common failure (e.g. missing env var or composer install failure).

events.note: Writing full scope (revision 2) for the remediation-panel extension.

events.change_summary: Full scope: add composer-install failure recognition + guided recovery to the deploy remediation catalog and panel. Single phase, no architecture/design.

events.rationale: Adds one new failure-recognition entry to the deploy remediation catalog plus tests. No new UI components or visual decisions, so skip design. Well-contained to config/remediations.php and a test file (1-2 files, builds entirely on the existing catalog/panel pattern), so skip architecture. Review is kept because the new fix runs a script as root over SSH and the matching regex must be verified against false positives.

events.decision: Cover composer (dependency) install failure as the new remediation type, served through the existing one-click `script` action path rather than a new bespoke inline "guided" component.
events.rationale: The catalog already covers redis/PDO/vhost/disk beyond the database case, and the panel is already generic over script/handler actions — so the genuinely-missing, common failure is a failed dependency install. Using a script action (like disk_full/phpredis) keeps the change to config + tests with no new UI, matching the starter scope. The out-of-memory case gets the actionable swap-ensuring fix; unresolvable-requirements is recognized for explanation only since swap won't fix a code/lockfile problem.

events.summary: Full scope (revision 2) is ready. It extends the deploy remediation catalog and panel to recognize composer (dependency) install failures — the genuinely-uncovered common case, since the catalog already handles redis/PDO/vhost/disk beyond the database failure. The new entry is served through the existing one-click script-action path (like disk_full/phpredis), so the change is config + tests with no new UI: an out-of-memory composer failure gets a recommended, idempotent swap-ensuring fix, while unresolvable-requirements is recognized for explanation only. Single phase, no architecture/design. 5 requirements, 6 acceptance criteria. Workflow: bug-fix / trivial (scope → implementation → review → release); review kept because the fix runs a script as root over SSH and the matching regex must be verified against false positives.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

Review is kept because the new fix runs a script as root over SSH and the matching regex must be verified against false positives.
